revisionChanges.change: Hero.css created with gradient rules using generic variables
revisionChanges.details: Hero.css exists with .top-gradient rules using --gradient-left, --gradient-right, and --accent-color. Background has 3 layers: linear-gradient with accent color, two radial-gradients with left/right gradient variables. Position absolute, height 150vw, z-index -10.

No .hero { } rules found in colors.css. Generic gradient system remains intact: --gradient-left and --gradient-right defined in :root and overridden per color class (.blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, .green).

revisionChanges.change: overflow-y: clip added to .wp-site-blocks in body.css
revisionChanges.details: .wp-site-blocks { overflow-y: clip; } found in body.css. This clips absolutely positioned gradients at the page content boundary without affecting scroll or creating new formatting contexts.

Test page has 11 spacer blocks (height:100vh, aria-hidden='true') placed inside DemoContainers after each hero block (except the last DemoContainer). This creates vertical spacing to make gradient bleed effect visible between blocks.

revisionChanges.change: PostHero inherits changes via shared 'hero' class
revisionChanges.details: PostHero.php line 29 uses class='post-hero hero relative overflow-visible', so it automatically inherits .top-gradient rules from Hero.css and benefits from overflow-y:clip on .wp-site-blocks. No PostHero file changes needed.

build.details: Build succeeded with only size warnings (340 KiB editor.js, expected)

checks.check: Hero.css created with generic gradient system
checks.details: .top-gradient uses --gradient-left, --gradient-right (from colors.css generic system) and --accent-color. 3-layer background: linear-gradient(accent), radial-gradient(left), radial-gradient(right). Absolute positioning, z-index -10, height 150vw.
No .hero { } rules in colors.css. Generic gradient variables (--gradient-left, --gradient-right) remain in :root with per-color overrides (.blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, .green).
Generic --gradient-left and --gradient-right variables found in colors.css with correct per-color overrides. System is reusable for any block using 'hero' class or other gradient needs.

overflowFix.details: Page overflow issue resolved while preserving gradient bleed

checks.details: body.css has .wp-site-blocks { overflow-y: clip; }. This clips absolutely positioned gradients at page boundary.
All 12 hero sections verified to have overflow-visible class. Gradient can still bleed between blocks within the .wp-site-blocks container.
Page audit confirms all 12 hero sections have .top-gradient elements present. Gradient height is 150vw, allowing bleed effect. Sections have overflow-visible to allow gradient to escape block bounds.
Raw content shows 11 wp:spacer blocks with height='100vh', placed inside DemoContainers after hero blocks. Spacers have aria-hidden='true' to hide from screen readers.
Each of the first 11 DemoContainers has a spacer after the hero block. Last DemoContainer has no spacer (no spacing needed after final block).

gradientRendering.details: Gradient system renders correctly on all hero variations

checks.details: Page audit confirms all 12 sections have .top-gradient element present (extraSelectors.top_gradient shows 12 true values)
Both hero--primary (9 instances) and hero--secondary (3 instances) render with gradients

postHeroInheritance.details: PostHero inherits gradient system via shared 'hero' class

checks.details: PostHero.php line 29 has class='post-hero hero relative overflow-visible', so it inherits .top-gradient CSS from Hero.css
PostHero automatically benefits from Hero.css (via shared class) and overflow-y:clip on .wp-site-blocks. No modifications required.

regressionChecks.details: No regression from previous PASS state

checks.details: Background images still have alt='', QuickNavigation aria-label fallback intact, aria-labelledby on sections preserved
24px chevron, texture overlay on QuickNavigation, decorative alt text, aria-label fallback all unchanged by CSS refactor
No TSX/PHP changes in this revision, sync remains intact

buildAndLint.details: Build passes, no new lint issues introduced by CSS changes

passedChecks: Build compiles successfully (CSS changes processed correctly)
Hero.css created with .top-gradient using generic --gradient-left/--gradient-right variables
colors.css hero-specific code removed (lines 143-206), generic gradient system intact
overflow-y: clip added to .wp-site-blocks in body.css
overflow-visible preserved on all 12 hero sections
All 12 hero sections render with .top-gradient elements
Test page has 11 spacers (100vh) inside DemoContainers for visual separation
PostHero inherits gradient system via shared 'hero' class (no changes needed)
Generic gradient system works for both primary and secondary hero variations
No regression: all previous QA fixes and accessibility improvements preserved

architectureImprovements.details: By moving hero gradient rules to Hero.css and relying on generic --gradient-left/--gradient-right from colors.css, the gradient system is now reusable by any block. PostHero already benefits from this via the shared 'hero' class.
overflow-y: clip on .wp-site-blocks is a site-wide fix that prevents any absolutely positioned elements from extending the page beyond content boundaries, not just hero gradients.

Removed 6 per-color .hero.X overrides from colors.css (neon-green, blue, green, yellow, orange, purple). The generic gradient system in colors.css handles color switching via class context automatically.

performanceNotes: CSS bundle size unchanged (gradient rules moved, not added)
overflow-y: clip does not create new stacking context or block formatting context
No JavaScript changes, no performance impact
